example_posting.title: Senior Software Engineer - ML Infrastructure
example_posting.snippet: Design, build and maintain training and serving infrastructure for ML research, including data pipelines for training and inference, distributed training systems for large models, and monitoring systems.

notes: Field experiencing 400% growth. Core focus on distributed training systems, inference optimization, and ML pipeline infrastructure. Often conflated with general DevOps but requires specific ML/LLM domain expertise.

example_posting.title: LLM Deployment Engineer
example_posting.snippet: Design and deploy on-prem LLM platforms, configure hardware and operating systems for AI workloads, run and maintain LLMs behind internal or customer-facing web services.

notes: Distinct from general LLM Engineer roles by focusing on deployment, operations, and infrastructure-level optimization. Key skills: RAG architecture, LLM-specific cloud platforms (Azure OpenAI, AWS), containerization, performance tuning.

example_posting.title: Sr. AI Site Reliability Engineer, AI.x
example_posting.snippet: Act as first responder for AI workload health and customer-facing issues. Build and support capabilities for managing ML workloads. Manage SLOs and incident response. Lead automation-first initiatives.

notes: SRE specialization for AI/ML systems. Bridges traditional SRE with ML observability, monitoring, and reliability engineering. Salary: $128K median, senior roles up to $276K. Distinct from MLOps Engineer by broader reliability focus.

example_posting.title: MLOps Engineer
example_posting.snippet: Deploy, monitor, and maintain machine learning models in production. Bridge gap between data science and operations by automating workflows, optimizing infrastructure, ensuring model reliability.

notes: Role title variance high (MLOps Engineer, Model Operations Manager, ML Ops Specialist). Core responsibilities: CI/CD for ML models, data pipeline management, performance monitoring, model deployment orchestration. Average salary $138-170K.

example_posting.title: Design Engineer
example_posting.snippet: Lead product design system focusing on UI component architecture, collaboration with teams, ensuring high-quality accessible designs for AI-native products.

notes: Distinct skill set combining design systems, frontend engineering, and AI product fluency. Design Engineer differs from AI Product Designer by emphasizing technical implementation and component architecture. Salary range $62K-$250K.

role_title: AI Product Designer / AI UX Designer

example_posting.title: Principal Product Designer
example_posting.snippet: Design breakthrough AI experiences including personal research agents, AI-powered content creation tools, 0->1 AI-native products. Mentor 3-4 senior designers. 10+ years product design, 3+ years designing AI/ML/LLM products.

notes: Emerging role requiring dual expertise: UX/UI design + ML/AI fluency. Key difference from traditional Product Designer: understanding model limitations, probabilistic outputs, and rapid iteration cycles. Salary: $110K-$440K.

example_posting.title: Prompt Experience Designer
example_posting.snippet: Design prompts for enhanced user experiences focusing on interactive artifacts and research workflows. Create evals and test prompts to create high-quality LLM experiences. Develop language-first content design methodologies.

notes: Unique role combining prompt engineering with content design and UX thinking. Distinct from Prompt Engineer by focusing on user experience and interaction patterns rather than individual prompt optimization. Attracts content designers with technical expertise.

example_posting.title: AI Prompt Engineer/Interaction Designer
example_posting.snippet: Combine machine learning, AI, and UX to improve interactions between users and AI assistants. Make interactions sound conversational and helpful, like interacting with a real person.

notes: Combines UX design with conversational AI expertise. Focus on dynamic, adaptive experiences where system learns and changes. Ensures AI behaves transparently and fairly. Early-stage adoption signal; emerging title variance.

example_posting.title: Design System Engineer
example_posting.snippet: Lead product design system focusing on UI component architecture, collaboration with teams, ensuring high-quality accessible designs for AI-native workflows.

notes: Specialized design systems role focused on AI-native product patterns. Limited distinction from traditional Design System Engineer roles focusing on AI as feature vs. AI as platform. Monitor for role differentiation.

role_title: AI Design Lead / Head of AI Design

example_posting.title: Product Design Lead, AI Products
example_posting.snippet: Lead design of breakthrough AI experiences including personal research agents, AI-powered content creation tools, 0->1 AI-native products. Mentor 3-4 senior designers. Requires 10+ years in product design, 3+ years designing AI/ML products.

notes: Leadership track for AI design specialists. Early-stage adoption; concentrated in Series B/C funded companies. Monitor for expansion into larger enterprises. Key requirement: deep LLM/AI product experience combined with design leadership.
